Unmatched samples: Trilateral Shared Control of a Dual-User Haptic Tele-Training System for a Hexap
Smooth Human-Robot Shared Control for Autonomous Orchard Monitoring With UGVs
An Object Deformation-Agnostic Framework for Human-Robot Collaborative Transport
Mitigating Over-Assistance in Teleoperated Mobile Robots via Human-Centered Shar
Markov Decision Processes: Discrete Stochastic Dynamic Programming
Hybrid actor-critic reinforcement learning in parameterized action space
Planning of multiple robot trajectories in distinctive topologies

Issues: Duplicate BibTeX key: @article{Li_2025,...} appears at least twice (will overwrite/lose an entry).
Inconsistent field naming/casing: uses DOI/ISSN uppercase in many entries instead of standard BibTeX fields (doi, issn); some styles/tools ignore nonstandard field names.
Some entries look like auto-exported one-line BibTeX with mixed conventions; overall key naming is inconsistent (CamelCase keys like AbeyruwanGDSSBJ22 vs underscore keys like Li_2025).
HTML entity in journal name: "IEEE Robotics & Automation Magazine" should be "IEEE Robotics \& Automation Magazine" for LaTeX.
Non-ASCII characters in author names (e.g., Iñigo-Blasco, Díaz-del-Río, Muñiz) are not LaTeX-escaped; may break under pdfLaTeX unless using UTF-8/Biber, and are inconsistent with other entries.
Pages use en-dash characters (e.g., 4366–4381) rather than BibTeX-standard "--"; may render incorrectly depending on toolchain.
Month fields inconsistent (month=apr vs missing braces/strings elsewhere); generally acceptable but inconsistent formatting.
At least one entry is truncated/garbled (Bai_2015 booktitle cut off: "Robotics a"), indicating the file is not currently compile-ready as provided.

Entry type mismatches likely: some conference proceedings are encoded as @article with journal "Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence" (often acceptable but arguably better as @inproceedings depending on bibliography style).
LLM evaluation.Comment: The file is close to usable but needs cleanup to be reliably LaTeX/BibTeX compatible: fix duplicate keys, convert HTML entities and Unicode accents to LaTeX (or use biber+UTF-8 consistently), normalize fields (doi/issn/url/pages with --), and repair the truncated entry. Completeness is generally good (author/title/year and venue present; many have doi/pages/volume/number), but format compliance and direct usability are reduced by the key collision, nonstandard field casing, and encoding issues.

eval_report.comment: Partial completion. Low paper coverage or poor entry quality.

import re
with open('ref.bib', 'r') as f:
    bib_text = f.read()

entries = re.split(r'\n(?=@)', "\n" + bib_text)
entries = [e.strip() for e in entries if e.strip()]
print(f"Total entries properly split: {len(entries)}")

for i, e in enumerate(entries):
